title: Teaching
text:
Applied Econometrics Reading Group Notes — Notes on Mostly Harmless Econometrics and Beyond. Developed with Fangzhou Yu and Yangqi Zhang for the Oz Metrics Reading Group at UNSW (2022–23, ongoing).
Topics covered: experimental ideal, sampling design, DAGs, sensitivity analysis, instrumental variables, synthetic control, regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences and event studies, and double machine learning (theory and applied).
Read the notes →

title: CV
text:
Working Papers & Publications

Legal aid eligibility and court outcomes: a design-based double-machine-learning approach.
Forthcoming, Journal of Law & Economics. TTPI WP.
Double-machine-learning approach with administrative data on Legal Aid applicants to estimate the effect of aid refusal on court outcomes.


An open repository of COVID-19 vaccine mandate studies with a worked scoping review of quasi-experimental evidence.
Lead author, with Blyth, C. and the MandEval team. Forthcoming, npj Vaccines. Article.
A dataset covering more than 500 papers on COVID-19 mandates, manually curated and with data extracted by an LLM, plus a worked scoping review of the quasi-experimental evidence on uptake and downstream outcomes.


Vaccinating Now or Vaccinating Later: separating pull-forward and net effects using a dynamic regression discontinuity design.
Lead author, with Attwell, K., Genie, M. and the MandEval team. Submitted. PDF.
Studies a novel COVID-19 vaccine mandate targeting graduating high-school students. A daily panel of linked administrative data (AIR-PLIDA) lets RDD run dynamically, disentangling intensive and extensive margins. Develops a toy model for external validity.


Career, Family, and IVF: the impact of involuntary childlessness and fertility treatment.
With Maryam Naghsh Nejad. IZA WP.
Dynamic triple DID approach to study the impacts of childlessness and IVF on women’s income and mental health, using Australian administrative data.


Conceiving naturally after IVF: the effect of an ART conception on obstetric outcomes.
With Zhang, X., Rombauts, L. and Chambers, G. arXiv.
Leverages idiosyncratic spontaneous pregnancy after a failed ART cycle to estimate the effect of ART on outcomes such as c-section and birth weight, using Australian administrative data (ANZARD).


Getting Explicit Instruction Right.
With Richard Holden. Submitted. arXiv.
Studies the dramatic effect of a public NSW school introducing the Explicit Instruction pedagogy, using synthetic control on administrative school data.


Grandmaternal health shocks, informal childcare, and maternal outcomes.
With Jan Kabatek. In progress.
Studies the effect of grandmaternal acute health episodes (identified by selected PBS drugs) on the labour outcomes of mothers with children aged 0–4.

title: Research
text:
Working Papers

Working paper
Career, Family, and IVF: The Impact of Involuntary Childlessness and Fertility Treatment
With Maryam Naghsh Nejad. Best Paper Award, 15th Australasian Workshop on Econometrics and Health Economics. Featured on the Grattan Institute’s Wonk List (2025).
Keywords: involuntary childlessness, IVF, mental health, labour market outcomes, fertility and career trade-offs. JEL: J13, J22, I14, I31, J16.

PDF



Working paper
Conceiving Naturally After IVF: the effect of an IVF conception on mode and timing of birth
With X. Zhang, L. Rombauts, and G. Chambers.
Keywords: fertility, assisted reproduction, IVF, obstetric outcomes, infertility. JEL: I10, I12, I19.
